description: Lord Strathclyde resigned as Leader of the House of Lords in January 2013 to pursue a career in business. Education He was educated at Wellington College, Berkshire, and the Universities of East Anglia and Aix-en-Provence. Political career Lord Strathclyde served as Leader of the House of Lords from May 2010 to January 2013. Before that he held several positions in government, including Minister of State at the Department of Trade and Industry and Government Chief Whip. He was also a member of the shadow cabinet between 1997 and 2010. He was made a Privy Counsellor in 1995. Career outside politics Although working professionally as a Lloyd’s insurance broker, he has attended the House regularly since his introduction in 1985. Personal life Lord Strathclyde is married with 3 children.

details.body:
Lord Strathclyde resigned as Leader of the House of Lords in January 2013 to pursue a career in business.

Education

He was educated at Wellington College, Berkshire, and the Universities of East Anglia and Aix-en-Provence.

Political career

Lord Strathclyde served as Leader of the House of Lords from May 2010 to January 2013. Before that he held several positions in government, including Minister of State at the Department of Trade and Industry and Government Chief Whip. He was also a member of the shadow cabinet between 1997 and 2010. He was made a Privy Counsellor in 1995.

Career outside politics

Although working professionally as a Lloyd’s insurance broker, he has attended the House regularly since his introduction in 1985.

Personal life

Lord Strathclyde is married with 3 children.

Lord Strathclyde resigned as Leader of the House of Lords in January 2013 to pursue a career in business.

Education

He was educated at Wellington College, Berkshire, and the Universities of East Anglia and Aix-en-Provence.

Political career

Lord Strathclyde served as Leader of the House of Lords from May 2010 to January 2013. Before that he held several positions in government, including Minister of State at the Department of Trade and Industry and Government Chief Whip. He was also a member of the shadow cabinet between 1997 and 2010. He was made a Privy Counsellor in 1995.

Career outside politics

Although working professionally as a Lloyd’s insurance broker, he has attended the House regularly since his introduction in 1985.

Personal life

Lord Strathclyde is married with 3 children.

The Leader of the House of Lords is responsible for the organisation of government business in the House, providing assistance to all Lords and offering advice on procedure. The Leader also expresses the collective feelings of the House on formal occasions, such as motions of thanks or congratulations.

From July 2014, this role is covered by the Leader of the House of Lords and the Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster.
